title: Per-ticket token budgeting and parallelism caps for fan-out delegation
source: Anthropic multi-agent research system — ~15x token usage finding & budget guidance (from prior knowledge; live fetch blocked)
description: Anthropic reported multi-agent systems consume roughly 15x the tokens of a single agent, and that explicit token/effort budgets per subagent are essential to keep cost bounded and prevent runaway workers. The CTO fans out Morty's (and parallel teams via team.py) without documented budget ceilings, so a single misbehaving worker can burn unbounded tokens. Adding a per-ticket budget derived from effort_score, plus a global concurrency cap, makes cost predictable and provides a hard stop.
proposed_changes: In workers.py add a `TokenBudget` helper that maps ticket effort_score to a max token/turn allowance and tracks cumulative usage from `claude -p` output (or a wall-clock/turn cap as proxy). In delegate.py pass `--max-turns`/budget to the subprocess and abort + emit a 'budget_exceeded' handoff (status=blocked) when exceeded. In team.py add a `MAX_CONCURRENT_MORTYS` semaphore so parallel fan-out never exceeds a configurable cap (default from .cto/config.json).
reject_reason: No files were actually modified (false success)
